title: AI-Assisted Supply Chain Attack Targets GitHub
summary: PRT-scan is the second campaign in recent months where a threat actor appears to have leveraged AI for automated targeting of a widespread GitHub misconfiguration.
email_summary: PRT-scan is the second campaign in recent months where a threat actor appears to have leveraged AI for automated targeting of a widespread GitHub misconfiguration. Severity is rated high.
card_summary: PRT-scan is the second campaign in recent months where a threat actor appears to have leveraged AI for automated targeting of a widespread GitHub misconfiguration.

title: Axios Attack Shows How Complex Social Engineering Is Industrialized
summary: The attack on the popular NPM package Axios is just one of many targeting maintainers and has shone a light on how threat actors can scale sophisticated social engineering campaigns.

sections.context: This reflects continued use of phishing as an initial-access path in broader intrusion chains.

email_summary: The attack on the popular NPM package Axios is just one of many targeting maintainers and has shone a light on how threat actors can scale sophisticated social engineering campaigns.
card_summary: The attack on the popular NPM package Axios is just one of many targeting maintainers and has shone a light on how threat actors can scale sophisticated social engineering campaigns.

title: Fortinet Issues Emergency Patch for FortiClient Zero-Day
summary: The authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-35616, is the latest in a series of Fortinet vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild.

sections.context: This story is part of the recurring zero-day pattern where exploitation pressure is highest before broad patch adoption.

email_summary: The authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-35616, is the latest in a series of Fortinet vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild. Tracked as CVE-2026-35616. Severity is rated critical.
card_summary: The authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-35616, is the latest in a series of Fortinet vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild.

title: Automated Credential Harvesting Campaign Exploits React2Shell Flaw
summary: An emerging threat cluster tracked as UAT-10608 is exploiting vulnerable Web-exposed Next.js apps and using an automated tool to exfiltrate credentials, secrets, and other system data.
email_summary: An emerging threat cluster tracked as UAT-10608 is exploiting vulnerable Web-exposed Next.js apps and using an automated tool to exfiltrate credentials, secrets, and other system data.
card_summary: An emerging threat cluster tracked as UAT-10608 is exploiting vulnerable Web-exposed Next.js apps and using an automated tool to exfiltrate credentials, secrets, and other system data.

title: Shadow AI in Healthcare Is Here to Stay
summary: Medical professionals are not going to stop using AI tools to manage growing workloads. Organizations should prioritize bolstering security protocols to limit their blast radius.
email_summary: Medical professionals are not going to stop using AI tools to manage growing workloads. Organizations should prioritize bolstering security protocols to limit their blast radius.
card_summary: Medical professionals are not going to stop using AI tools to manage growing workloads. Organizations should prioritize bolstering security protocols to limit their blast radius.

title: OWASP GenAI Security Project Gets Update, New Tools Matrix
summary: In recognition of 21 generative AI risks, the standards group recommends that companies take separate but linked approaches to defending GenAI and agentic AI systems.
email_summary: In recognition of 21 generative AI risks, the standards group recommends that companies take separate but linked approaches to defending GenAI and agentic AI systems.
card_summary: In recognition of 21 generative AI risks, the standards group recommends that companies take separate but linked approaches to defending GenAI and agentic AI systems.

title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
summary: An elusive hacker who went by the handle UNKN and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

sections.overview: An elusive hacker who went by the handle UNKN and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.
sections.context:
This aligns with ongoing ransomware campaigns targeting operational continuity and recovery readiness.

This fits the broader data-exposure trend where follow-on abuse of leaked information is common.
